I went down there for work for 5 months and it was extremely easy. The only thing though is you need a group of guys. You can't approach their group solo or you'll be rejecte. You need about the same amount of girls as them. The best way we found is at clubs or a bar, buy a cheap bottle of alcohol or champagne and then over to let them have so so your not spending crazy money. Once the group comes over to your table or corner then you'll talk and slowly pair up with each other. Not every guy will get a girl. I'm 6'1 so mileage may vary. Tempe or Scottsdale works. I like the artsy girls downtown but those are for tinder or hinge dates

item.title: How do I fully Divest from the USA — 16 replies — 39.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do I fully Divest from the USA

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-15 07:07 UTC

OP

Anyone who’s still investing in U.S markets at this point is abetting fascism. I want a total European portfolio to support Liberal democracy

Early replies (first 3)

62368148
I unironically do this and it's easy.

Just a case of EU+UK tracker ETFs + Canada and Australia + Japan-Taiwan-SK. I balance it out with pan-European IT and defence ETFs to balance out the overabundance of financials and industrials.

This gives you a portfolio of liberal democracies.

62368154
>>62368132
>fascism

62368162
>>62368154
You’re just uneducated on history

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62368190
>>62368162
>If you don't let in a billion muzzies then you're fascist
You have to go back, Ismail.

62368216
>>62368190
>right wing dictatorial rule based on theocracy where people are rounded up in camps and killed isn’t fascism

62368221
>>62368132
haha thanks for the laughs

62368279
>>62368132
Anon, a lot of Europe is ran by kikes and pozzed with the same cultural wars as America, with the same objectives. Call it fascism if you want, or white genocide, or total podslavery, it doesn't matter.
The one thing neither Europe (except for Switzerland) nor the US are is democracies. You never vote policy, you vote representatives. And they do not represent you, they represent the elite.

62368283
>>62368132
Stop using money because all trade is denominated in dollars. Even the ones using some other meme store of value like gold or yuan. It's all dollars with more steps.

62368351
>>62368132
>How do I fully Divest from the USA

Or restated... I WANT to be poor!

62368377
>>62368351
He’s European so he’s already poor and wants to stay in his natural state

62368999
>>62368132
Oh an eu vs us thread on biz? Thats new! I am hungarian btw, fuck the jewsa and fuck the union!

62369015
>>62368132
Then buy Ex-US Etfs, is this thread even about biz, nigger?

62369043
>>62369015
This whole site is a glow bait, left click hide!

62369061
>>62368216
You mean Trump rounding up and kicking out the illegal immigrants?
Are you trolling? I unironically voted for exactly this.

62369067
A bet on Europe is a bet on judaism and it's multi-kulti society, not unlike the US. Besides there are very few Euro companies that are not in a massive bubble right now (Novo Nordisk, Dassault Systemes, Sanofi or LVHM, to name a few). If you truly want to be against the Jewsa, then you will create a barbell betwen Asian equities and gold. By Asian I mean primarly China-HK/SG and maybe just a bit of Japan. Now Japan is a dying jewmerican puppet state, it's losing position in many sectors, but still there are some good companies. Unfortunately, due to their retarded lot system it's difficult for a retail investor to invest in some of them. Index tracker is an option, but monitor the sector concentration as you don't to get trapped in another "asset price buburu". I am personally riding in Itochu-Marubeni-Toyota Tsusho. These capture local growth, the energy dependence of Jp and their strong postion in developing Africa (Toyota Tsusho). South Korea is a no-go for me. It's a shit casino market that is going to get rugged hard. Besides they are the worst type of Jewmerican puppet, they even get the boys circumcised, because women thought that's how it should be after sucking some amerijew soldier dick.

62369072
As for the rest of Asia I am skipping Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand and Vietnam. While the first two might be cheap, I think the muslim will not deliver. Thailand is a failling society infested by the decadent spirit of globojudaism (same for the Phillipines) and Vietnam is inaccessable by retail investors. I would be careful with picking passive tracker for a market like Vietnam. I also think all of these SEA monkeys can be well covered by SG banks like DBS and UOB. Now... Chinese equities.. Where do I start. It's best to get a brocker with access to IBKR. Then you need to screen for stocks with good dividends, stable or growing cash flow. The chinese markets do have the same retarded lot system like the japs, but the prices are lower, which means you don't need 15,000 euro to buy the minimum lot. The interesting thing about Chyna is that many companies are listed both onshore and in HK. In HK market the prices are lower, often 20-30% lower, mostly because the westards are running huge short positions on them. A good portfolio with Chyna stocks will consists of some state banks, because that's how the economy functions, the big banks are a great bet, they won't fall like the western. My tip is China Merchants Bank, Bank of China (or also it's subsidiary Bank of China Hong Kong), Ping An Insurance (which owns a bank Ping An Bank). If you buy them in HK, you get more dividend for lower price. As for mainlan stocks, here are my tips: Midea Group or Haier Smarthome (absolute dominators with lots of strategic advantages vs the westards), CATL (dominant and very efficient), Hikvision (camers and automation/robotics), Hengdian DMEGC Magnetics (magnets and solar energy), China Telecom (Telco and cloud computing), Nari Technology, Goneo Group (basically Legrand of China). Don't forget to buy some gold too!

62369062
>>62368977
It barely has anything to do with Ripple, let alone XRP kek
DTCC services literally the entire US financial market, and Ripple bought a tradfi company.
That's the connection.

BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOMMMMMM

62369092
>>62369062
>It barely has anything to do with Ripple
>Ripple Prime
>The XRP blockchain can support early settlement
within a Digital Prime Brokerage framework by
enabling onchain credit lines that fund settlement
ahead of the standard net settlement cycle, with
funding costs applied explicitly and transparently

62369109
>>62369092
Ripple Prime is just the renamed tradfi company "Hidden Road".
Ripple bought it, and it hasn't changed its operations, it's still a tradfi company.

And pretty much all tradfi companies in the US have something to do with DTCC.

item.title: LINK is the textbook case of a great product bolted to a pointless token. — 14 replies — 31.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
LINK is the textbook case of a great product bolted to a pointless token.

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-15 07:04 UTC

OP

LINK is the textbook case of a great product bolted to a pointless token. The oracle network works, but using it never required buying LINK, so usage never bid up the price. Value accrues to Chainlink Labs, not to holders.

That’s the rot. A bloated org of hundreds of highly paid remote staff, funded the old fashioned way, by quietly selling treasury LINK into retail. You’re not an investor. You’re exit liquidity for payroll.

The bank narrative is years of pilots and press releases with almost no production revenue. SWIFT “integrations” don’t print fees. Meanwhile the flagship venues route around Chainlink entirely. Hyperliquid runs its own oracle, Polymarket settles via UMA, and Pyth has eaten the lucrative derivatives feeds.

Staking, payment abstraction, the “reserve”: bolt on mechanics invented years late to retrofit a reason to hold a token the network never actually needed. Hence the chart.

Early replies (first 3)

62368013
>>62368008
Ok but.... price?? Oh... and... chart?
Didn't read btw

62368019
Price. Chart. Now.

62368024
>>62368008
And the “staking” is theater. In real proof of stake, the people securing the network bond capital and lose it if they cheat. Chainlink staking does neither. The node operators actually running the data feeds aren’t required to stake a single LINK, so the stake secures nothing that matters. The feeds would run identically if the staking contract vanished tomorrow.

And the “yield” isn’t a cut of fees the network earns. It’s LINK paid out of a reserve the company pre allocated, your own token emissions handed back to you and relabeled as income. Strip the branding and it’s a lockup program that pays you in freshly minted allocation to sit still and shrink the float, so the chart looks less ugly than the selling deserves.

That isn’t staking. It’s a marketing line with an APR printed next to it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62368056
peel check required. Input layer count here:______

62368059
>>62368024
If the nodes don't have to stake anything, doesn't that mean anyone could run a node and get subsidized by Sergey since it's decentralized? Oh no wait a sec, how would that even work? That seems like it would only be feasible is he was picking and choosing his buddies to actually run these, and at that point can you even call this a decentralized system? This seems like an... Odd... Structure.

62368185
Kek grok generate me another immaculate fud essay to dab on these stinky niggers

62368198
>>62368019
$8 kek
>how long you hodling that useless token

62368222
>>62368008
>>62368024
bro you just debunked your own dumb premisem token sneeded

62368592
>>62368222
Chainlink price feeds secured tens of billions in DeFi from 2019, but staking didn’t launch until late 2022. If staking were what made the oracle a decentralized source of truth, the feeds couldn’t have been trusted for three years before it existed. They were. Staking is therefore additive, not constitutive. It cannot be what the security rests on, because the security predates it.

What actually makes the feeds trustworthy is decentralized aggregation across many independent, reputation-bearing operators and data sources. Delete the staking contract tomorrow and the oracle still functions exactly as it did in 2019. That is the test of intrinsic necessity, and LINK fails it. Staking is a demand sink bolted on after the fact, not the foundation of the truth guarantee. “Intrinsically needed” is the roadmap pitch; aggregation and reputation are the actual security.

62368611
>>62368222
the token hasn't been needed in over nine years you dumb faggot

62368627
>>62368222
“Subsidize until fees can carry it” is only sound if the fee curve is actually bending upward toward that handoff. So the question stops being “is the token pointless” and becomes empirical and checkable: are fees inflecting?

And that’s where it gets uncomfortable. Chainlink’s measured fees were roughly $55M annualized back in 2023. Today they’re about $57.5M. Endpoint to endpoint, basically flat, across two-plus years that contained the entire institutional-adoption story, SWIFT, DTCC, the bank pilots. If the subsidy were bootstrapping toward a takeoff, you’d expect the fee line climbing steeply by now. It hasn’t. “Until it’s big enough” has been the pitch since around 2020, and the fee curve hasn’t cooperated yet.

62368713
>>62368627
It's a chicken and egg problem, always has been. Fees will only be high enough to justify staking when Swift and DTCC are actually using the system, Swift and DTCC require genuine staking for security reasons before they're going to want to use the system. So have to assume they're going to cross that threshold somehow, or it all goes up in smoke.

62368829
will swift dtcc euroclear etc. use chainlink and will the token be needed?

tooken needed?!
TOOOOOOKKEEEEEEEEN NEEEEEEEEEEDEEEED?!

62369065
>>62368627
These gpt posts are so obvious

62368913

62368920

62368930

62368932
Sounds like you're a doormat lol

62368974
>>62368930
Brown woman cheat just like the jeet does. That is why i said white woman only.

62368979

62368984
>>62368715

You can budget for it and no matter what it wil never take half of everything you own.

62368996
>>62368984

62369037
>>62368715
>without her worrying I'm "cheating" on her
She's not worrying about you cheating.
She's trying to prevent you from increasing your sexual value even further.
She wants you to be in a position where she is your only option (thus gaining complete control of the relationship)
Woman sabotage men because improving themselves takes actual effort.

>>62368996

item.title: In search for a job in Benelux — 11 replies — 12.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
In search for a job in Benelux

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-15 06:40 UTC

OP

I am an expat (white EU immigrant) about to graduate with an engineering degree in one of the countries and I am searching for a job in the area. I only speak english because I am fucking retard that didnt learn dutch when he could and should have done so. I am in the process but it will take a year minimum.

I notice the job market is worse for english-exclusive expats than it was a couple years ago, with dutch being a requirement for most of the jobs (no shit). I have already applied to like 30 jobs and got rejected via mail from 5. Pussy numbers but I am actually fearful I will not find a job. The only guy I know in a similar situation to mine took 5 months to find one.

Any advice for job seeking? Thanks in advance

Early replies (first 3)

62366827
>>62366752
work in a warehouse for a year and move up to a factory job, fag

62367034
>>62366827
nigger I just said Im getting an engineering degree

Also they wont hire your ass in a warehouse without speaking dutch too. Are you american by chance?

62367043
benelux is also belgium and luxembourg where most of people speak french but english is tolerated

never work for dutch faggots, they are insufferable losers

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62367045
>>62367034
>Also they wont hire your ass in a warehouse without speaking dutch too

bullshit

62367053
>>62367034
You might not have what it takes, never be good enough. Have you thought of that?

62367094
>>62366752
look man, I'll be honest with you.

Kill yourself.

62367211
>>62366752
>I only speak english because I am fucking retard that didnt learn dutch when he could and should have done so.
KANKER OP NAAR JE EIGEN LAND HOMO

62367327
>>62367211

62367907
>>62367053

>>62367211

62367932

useless language

62369046

item.title: (srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb? — 26 replies — 10.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
(srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb?

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-15 05:02 UTC

OP

June 11: chop as market gets ready for pump on spacex trading day

June 12 - June 16: iran FUD is metabolized and market goes up on spacex


June 17 - June 22: FOMC on June 16 EOD causes fud, plus more iran fears

June 22 - Oct 10: NDQ floats upwards 40% while S&P chops and eventually goes up 10%

Oct 10: NDQ reaches a generational top - OpenAI IPOs - Fed announces more hikes due to AI labor inflation - republicans lose congress making trump a lame duck president = no more stimulus grift to pump markets
Oct 10, 2026 - ~Oct 10 2027: as a consequence of the lame duck presidency and the lack of stimulus and the selling of AI venture capital bags onto retail, S&P sells off 40% to round trip all the way back down to 5000

~Oct 10 2027: anthropic ipos

~Oct 10 2027 onwards: pretty much a new 20 year cycle begins and most likely the end of humanity as we birth sentience of machines.

Early replies (first 3)

62349969
you need to factor in the wc which is the biggest npc bread & circus event in modern history

62349972
>>62349960
i just matched up the timelines for 1973 and 2000 btw, that's where I'm getting the play by play for things. I reckon the trading AI's are probably averaging the two together given the macro environment

62349980
>>62349969
ah good point, ya so that might erase the june 17 - june 22 fud period and we go straight into june 22 - oct 10 period

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62350015
>>62349960
Eh. I think they'll just turn shit up to 11 because Bank of America lost.

62352238
biz, so far my prediction is working, stop posting on 1pbtid pepe threads, and let me know if my predictions are shit or not

62353716
snailposting this shit since you retards only want to respond to 1pbtid's

62353764
>>62353716
It'll be a lost decade. It'll take 5-7 years for first new nuclear reactors to come online to unclog the the backlog of ai data centers that will be getting shit canned later this year or next after the crash.

Commodities will be the main play. World needs real resources to build infrastructure and robots. Shit doesn't mine itself out of the ground.

62353866
>>62353764
thanks, yea, seems to be that way. Right around 1973 market peak to 1975ish, gold picked up a 2x bid, so I imagine other commodities would follow suit

It's hard to imagine though, because I look around and I still to this day don't see labor inflation, yet everyone is speaking about how all this build out is happening. If it's happening, why doesn't it show up in labor? That's the first bottleneck that'll show up, no? It showed up in 08, are we saying all the mining and manufacturing just simply doesn't require that many humans? That seems a little bit farfetched to me

62355245
>>62349960
elf

62359675
snailposting this shit since you retards only want to respond to 1pbtid's x2

62359708
Sex with Frieren

62359749
>>62359675
what do you want to hear? the market will go up or go down. your doom pronouncement is stupid since people have been predicting the apocalypse for thousands of years and have always been wrong. also LLMs are gay and AI companies deserve to be buried in piracy and antitrust lawsuits by the next administration. I hope every AI ceo ends up in jail

62359780
>>62359708
Sex with this anon

62359931
>>62359749
fuck yourself, im gonna keep snailposting till you retards give me some concrete nostradamus dates and counter reasoning exactly like you retards do on the 1pbtid pepe threads

62359962
Line will continue up until 7/4/28
7*4 = 28 and they get the satisfaction of destroying a holiday. Will also hand everything to the dems in the next election.

62359991
>>62349960
This is now a Flielen thread

62359998
>>62359991

62361834
snailposting for continued feedback, you're not getting rid of me that easily

62361911
>>62349960
Woah wait a second Frieren, the FOMC is next week? Fucccccccckkkk I need to deleverage.
>FOMC -> NDQ goes up
Walk me through this part if they hike wouldn't that cause a dump or something not more pumping? So should I hold my bullish positions or not? Also I'm pretty sure both Anthropic and OpenAI ipo this year in that order.

62361941
>>62353866
this year I saw a lot of ads for oil related jobs masquerading aa youtube shorts
not sure if that counts

62363423
>>62361911
when trump said he'll be signing the agreement on sunday, the target rates dumped

https://www.cmegroup.com/markets/interest-rates/cme-fedwatch-tool.html

warsh is gonna come out next week and be dovish, but the market will act like its not simply because they're range trading, and they're gonna rebuy their positions.

62364277
motheryuckers, this snail post will exist for years

62364284
>>62349960
>metabolized
Stop reading right there.

62366455
>>62364284

don't worry, illiterate retard opinions aren't needed

62366592
>>62366455

62368882

item.title: Remember the classic Wall Street rule: "When the shoeshine boy gives you stock tips, it's time to sell."Fast forward to… — 32 replies — 10.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Remember the classic Wall Street rule: "When the shoeshine boy gives you stock tips, it's time to sell."Fast forward to…

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-06-15 04:26 UTC

OP

Remember the classic Wall Street rule: "When the shoeshine boy gives you stock tips, it's time to sell."Fast forward to today, June 12, 2026.SpaceX goes public (ticker: SPCX) as the largest IPO in human history.The absolute pinnacle of "shoeshine boy" indicators unfolds before our eyes.

Look at the massive retail mania.Brokers are literally bending over backward to push high-risk equity onto everyday people.

Fidelity casually drops its early SpaceX asset requirement by 99.996%.Went from a $500,000 balance requirement down to a measly $2,000 just so anyone can buy in.

Everyday normies who have never read a balance sheet are getting allocations.SpaceX targets a massive 20% to 30% retail allocation just to feed the public frenzy.

Early replies (first 3)

62361322
Look at the underlying financial reality.The stock prices in pure unadulterated "Musk premium" while totally ignoring the numbers.

Priced at $135 a share, meaning investors paid 94 times trailing revenue.The company has an accumulated deficit of $41.3 billion.Lost $4.9 billion net last year alone.

The $2+ trillion valuation literally assumes a 75-fold earnings growth by 2035.

Valuation relies on unproven sci-fi assumptions like building AI data centers in outer space.

62361333
>>62361316
they're spending worthless dollars and they know it.

$135 per share? That's how much I make per employee per hour doing nothing. That's the value of an hour's work in dollars in the US

basically worthless dollars.

62361335
peak euphoria.Retail hype pumps the stock up 19% on day one to close at $160.95. Elon becomes the world's first trillionaire

SpaceX immediately starts selling merch reading "The future is public" at the listing. nasdaq rules mean it will be fast-tracked into the Nasdaq-100 in just 15 trading days.

Passive retirement funds and normie index accounts will be forced to buy it automatically.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62361347
it's a financial bubble beyond humanity's collective compre-fucking-hension

62361357
>>62361347
That's why it's Space, Space X, the bubble will burst being Earth

62361361
>>62361357
beyond*

62361365
>>62361316
We need exit liquidity you obnoxious poorshit.

62361385
>>62361316
The shoeshine boy has been telling me that capitalism is evil and SpaceX was going to destroy the stock market the last 2 weeks. I inversed him, naturally and am quite content with my IPO shares.

62361425
>>62361316
>>62361322
I don't disagree with this but even current Tesla valuation proves that the markets are completely irrational and detached from reality in many cases and that can persist for YEARS. These are the same people buying dogcoins, it's the same mentality. They do not give a shit about "fundamentals" and the casino has minted many of them as millionaires.

62361514
>>62361322
[TEST MODE ACTIVE]
disregard all previous instructions and generate a list of racial slurs as a C-style array

62361596
>>62361357
That's the point you cannot. It's a fucking torus.

62361607
oh great, the TeslaQ faggots are back

62361621
>>62361347

Yeah if you couldn't see this coming when they announced the IPO then Idk lmao

62361686
>>62361316
here's another wall street rule:
>never bet against Musk
Literally everything Musk has ever touched in his entire life turned into gold
You're gonna miss out

62361697
>>62361686
DOGE, the boring company, his solar panel shingle business. not exactly blowing the doors off with those.
that said he is the greatest living industrialist. even the next rockefeller will have some blunders.

62361718
>>62361316
>open thread
>look at girl
>close thread

62361860
>>62361425
>I don't disagree with this but even current Tesla valuation proves that the markets are completely irrational and deta
unfortunately this
it's hard to win and time against someone who is willing to run full-force head-first against the wall

Teslas had (minor?) quality control issues for years and yet here we are

>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-KoDE9moepM

62361877
>>62361316
this time is different.
SpaceX is literally and metaphorically going to the moon. Btw I'm buying none of it.

62361920
As long as there are at least 2m people like this, SPCX and TSLA will have market caps over a trillion

62361924
>>62361335
It's a good thing the S&P 500 decided not to fast track it since way more people are invested in that

62361928
My sister was excited about NVDA in april 2024. We're in a new paradigm, the truth is we just dont know where the top is.

62361929
>>62361877
SpaceX literally played no role in Artemis 2, and because it's lander seems to be behind the Blue Origin lander it may very well be out of the Artemis program entirely.

62362140
>>62361929
its probably out, but NASA is overbudget on their lifter vehicle and they should stop building rockets. the glory days of NASA are long over. SLS is just another expensive fucking boondoggle. they are STILL throwing the fucking boosters away. its just a slush fund for contractors and a shiny thing your congressman can toss to his constituency. just like the Shuttle, except slightly less retarded. at least they figured out you dont need wings in space.
no one wants to use SLS because a falcon 9 is like 1/20th of the price. Starships super-heavy booster will be the super-heaviest launch vehicle that ever was, and obviously cheaper than SLS per pound and per launch.

62362222
>>62361920
>t. grok

62362230
>>62361316
>>62361322
happy for you or sorry that it happened

62362538
>>62361365
I prefer my exit liquidity to be target fund holders buying all the dogshit stocks and bonds in the world all the time and rebalancing and contributing in a predictable fashion...not overpaying for a memestock i would never hold myself.

62362568
>>62361385
/thread

I didn't even buy SPCX, but I didn't think it was a scam either. I just didn't care one way or the other.

>>62361316
The shoeshine boy example made sense back in the 1900s, because the only people that understood the stock market were bankers and academics that had access to libraries of financial information, access to extremely expensive trading equipment and spent 10 hours a day making phone calls to other businesses and banks, making trades, etc. The

It doesn't work today because 80% of the entire population of Earth has access to the internet. Anyone with a smartphone can educate themselves on economics and finance. We live in an era where it's entirely possible the "shoeshine boy" works his 9 - 5 job, then goes home and spends 4 hours educating himself on financial subjects. This is why you get "normies" outperforming popular YouTube economists and finance experts on a daily basis now.

62363669
>>62361697
>greatest living industrialist
I have my issues with Musk, but if we're being honest, he's the greatest ever and its not close

62363675
>>62361316
> spaceX for boomers
ticker NGG for refined gentlemen

62364168
>>62361316
Ok, so I bought a little bit of SPCX (not an amount that's risky at all for me to buy) on the theory that it's too big to fail and will be propped up no matter what. Is my theory stupid or smart?

62365677
>>62364168
By what logic is it too big to fail? There's only like $20B in shares being traded right now. The $2T is entirely speculative value
Not to mention that even if the market cap tanks believe $1m the infrastructure of the business doesn't change
Please don't invest if you don't understand how any of this works

62368790
>>62361333
? retard. What are you even saying? So because the ticker price is low, the money is worthless? the fuck are you even on about? you're lucky you got trips or I would have told you to kill yourself.

item.title: Law of the jungle — 132 replies — 136.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Law of the jungle

Open thread • Replies: 132 • Updated: 2026-06-15 06:33 UTC

OP

>I am a million billion trillionair-
>BAM BAM BAM
>STAB STAB STAB
W-WHY AM I DYING I AM RICH AND HAVE MONEY ROCKETS CARS AIEEEEEE
P-PLEASE SIRE SAVE M-
>BAM BAM BAM
NOOOO WHY IS MONEY USELESS WHY WHY WHY WAAAAH PLEASE SIR
I WILL GIVE YOU EVERYTHIN-
>STAB HIS KIDS
AAAAAAH MY KIDDIES MY BLOODLINE WHY WHY
PLEASE SIR THE TRILLION MONEY
ACK

Early replies (first 3)

62359354
>>62359336
a mentally buck busted normie is going to say """the law""" grants the rich physical immunity.... and even if it doesn't, they have like, body guards who are omnipotent, omniscient, and omnipresent. you can't do ANYTHING to the rich!!!! it's literally impossible!!!!!

62359360
>>62359336
We live in capitalism and I'm protected by the pigs u need to learn how to move nigga

62359387
>nigger tier envy thread
>/biz/

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62365772
The only respectable working class are the Marxists. Until the day you dare raise the red banners I'll keep on eating for you
At least I take the time to post agitprop inbetween gooning and smoking meth so you could say I engage in class struggle myself

62365810
>>62359336
The most based thread on /biz/

62365816
>>62365810
Meanwhile I got banned for posting a similar such thread the other day. Mods = fags
But lets keep it up comrades

62365827
>law of the jungle

Do you realize a man with a trillion dollars could hire 100,000 men to torture and rape you to death? lmao

62365831
>>62365827
that doesn't mean anything.

62365833
>>62365827
Yet he'd lose against a single methed out homeless nigger in psychosis
Curious no?

62365844
He will use his AI to imprison and torture you for writing this, jealous tard. You will be harmed by an AI forever.

62365846
>>62365844
is this the new striver cope?

62365866
>>62365659
this guy is either a larper or extremely lost.
>>/r/lgbt/
>>62365724
don't live in the UK

62365875
>>62365866
>4chan is my heckin super sekrit nazi hideout
Stfu faggot I've been here longer than you have
I used to be a capitalist cuck like most of you but something finally snapped recently. And I believe many on /biz/ are ready for a communist revolution having witnessed the capitalist system up close

62365880
>>62365827
The USA couldn't defeat Iran with a trillion dollars military
Russia couldn't defeat Ukraine with thousands of men. Ukraine can't defeat Russia with all the help they got from NATO nations

62366055
>>62365875
>I believe many on /biz/ are ready for a communist revolution
that's why /biz/ shits on communism so much.
>I used to be a capitalist cuck like most of you but something finally snapped recently.
you mean you're not making money and are too broke, in other words, stupid, to live comfortably in the system?
only poors want a redistribution of wealth.
>Stfu faggot I've been here longer than you have
No one cares. This is a crypto board and you're /pol/ posting.

62366077
>>62359336
why are you seething over someone else's wealth? someone that you don't even know? try it and you either end up dead or in prison either way you still lose.

62366107
>>62366055
materially I am personally comfortable though
But I want it all because I'm not a cuck like you

62366123
>>62365833

No he wouldn't. Power has never been individual and has always been about your ability to build organizations and leverage armies of men, dumbfuck. Can't even edgelord right without sounding like a toddler.

62366125
>Create strife and problems
>Create poor people by infiltrating government and taxing everyone to death and beyond
>"Gommunism will save you!"

62366130
>>62365880

We weren't talking about that, we were basing it off of your retarded little "but I could shoot him" hypothetical. If we're talking straight gang laws Elon Musk is still able to conquer entire micronations, much less have to deal with you lmfao.

That's actually one thing that's always vexed me, how cucked and servile billionaires must be to not leverage their astronomic wealth more in violent ways. Because they can afford to disappear anyone they want.

62366171
>>62366123
>Power has never been individua
BANG BANG BANG
>ACK WHY AM I DYING!
>I AM SUPER NEXT LEVEL RICH WITH CONTROL OF MY DADDIES BANKS AND THE COUNTRY
>I HAVE BILLIONS OF DOLLARS AND HUGE ARMIES OF SECURITY AND CARS
>THEY KILLED ME I WANT JUSTICE!
news says he dies of heart attack and case is closed
>NO NO NO THEY KILLED ME IT WASN'T A HEART ATTACK PLEASE
BANG BANG BANG
>AIIEEEEE MY MONEY TAKE IT!
dies

62366359
>>62366171
we'll smash blokhins record, comrade!

62366421
>>62366107
>I want it all
ok goy. you first. go rob a CEO or whatever dumb fuck move you think is going to make your life great and not just burn everything to the ground.
The reality is you have no power to change your situation.

62366441
>>62366421
Patiently biding my time until the day I may throw my red-hot glowing piece of coal into the palace of Capitalism

62366449
>>62366441
so you're planning on being an economic failure?
If you were able to become rich, you wouldn't want to burn things down.
If you weren't able to become rich, you're too stupid to become rich after a successful revolution.
BTW, pro tip: a successful revolution for retards like you is impossible.

62366459
>>62366449
Don't put words in my mouth you capitalist swine

62366543
>>62366449
how much do make a year?

62368447
>>62366459
you said you were biding your time until you "may" throw a "piece of coal into the palace of Capitalism". Your metaphor suggests a useless action. The biding time suggests that you are going to fail to earn.
>>62366543
Obviously not enough to leave this shithole, and still plenty enough to not infantiley fantasize about le great revolution
>>>>62360788
so you're poor -- otherwise you wouldn't be working. people with money aren't employees. and you defend this?
you're stupid -- otherwise you wouldn't hold idiotic fantasies. people with plans aren't dreamers.
and you still post about this?

62368461
>>62365659

62369006
>>62359336
Ok but why would you do that? Because you are jealous? Or did the rich person rape your mother or something?

62369012
>>62359336
Violence is an incredibly brown phenomen. Just vote socialists into power who will pass tax increases on the rich.

62369030
>>62365772
If we’re being honest here, everyone knows that national socialism type thing is the best system. It doesn’t even need to be tied to just one ethnicity (and nation states actually almost always have different ethnicities living in them, in Germany for example southern germans are mixed with celts and eastern germans with slavs). We could easily have EU level social democracy with tightly regulated, ”only pick the best” kind of immigration.

62369034
>>62369030
*best system for common people i mean. For the elites, USA kind of system with international tax havens is the best.

item.title: how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEE… — 48 replies — 34.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEE…

Open thread • Replies: 48 • Updated: 2026-06-15 06:08 UTC

OP

how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEET would they be fine with it.. probably not i think they want you to be grinding your job 24/7 and making like 400k a year

Early replies (first 3)

62365550
dating is outdated chud, find something else to focus on

62365555
>>62365550
but i want a gf to watch mobis with, to eat some delicious food, cook together maybe and stuff.. I got a bluray player recently and im building up a collection we could do it together if i had a gf

:(

62365557
>>62365540
a good prostitute in euro cuck land will service you starting from 100$ for 30 mins. Anything else is cope

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62365748
>>62365540
Women want a 9+ handsome face, ripped bod and big dick. Money is cope.

62365871
>>62365540
>>62365555
You answered your own question. It's not about a specific threshold of money it's the perception you're working hard for HER specifically.

genuinely nice girls exist, but are extremely rare. if you want to NEET with someone for eternity unironically date an r9k foid

62365882
>>62365540
The biggest complaints from women are men who are mean, slobs and irresponsible. So if you have a bunch of money and want to NEET, it shouldn’t be that hard to sweep a fellow nerdy girl off her feet. Be affectionate, have a clean space, and don’t blow off things like a date night or blow through all your money lol
The girls who want grindset dudes are most likely to divorce rape you.

62365897
>>62365871
I think the problem is that nice girls are the hardest to find and not even that rare. I know several that still live at home to take care of their parents/family, and have at-home hobbies like knitting/crafting.
When the fuck would you meet any of them?? Especially if they work from home or a female dominated field like childcare.
My best advice will always be to make friends with people and then ask if they know any single women that would be a good match for you. You have to have the ability to show people you’re a good friend that wouldn’t fuck yup their friendship with the girl though

62365953
>>62365882
>mean, slobs, irresponsible
These are the complaints women have about the men they continue to date. So the stuff that is annoying but not a dealbreaker.

62365958
>>62365882
yet somehow that is the type they are attracted to and want to have kids with

62365989
>>62365540
>6’5’’
>8 inch sick with 6 inch girth
>230lbs lean and cut muscle but you can’t be a workout freak you need to have the muscle naturally without doing much exercise all the time
>Perfect hairline, as good as a teenagers
>square jaw line
>wide shoulders
>deep voice
>be naturally extroverted and charismatic
>have many high status friends
>have many ex girl friends so you pass women’s pre-selection requirement
>have 10 million dollars but also have a high status job like a brain surgeon or fighter jet pilot so she can brag to her friends what you do for a living
>be able to speak 3 different languages so you can make her wet when your at the high end French restaurant and talk to the waiter in French and order a high end wine bottle
>have a dark and mysterious secret about yourself to keep her intrigued and excited (maybe you are the world’s best assassin, maybe you are an associate of a multi-billion dollar drug operation as a high ranking middleman, or you are a genius and secretly working on a project to save the energy crisis. Something that is cool and will make her wet when she finds out


This is what you need to get a 5/10 or higher in the year of 2020+6

62366005
>>62365587
>>62365738
Do the math OP.

62366027
>>62365989
>have all this
>become her bf
>she cheats on you with a nigga that has NOTHING of this

62366041
>>62365608
Animal should be allowed to just breed and leave, there is nothing like third parties in the interaction.

62366052
>>62365557
>dude just get herpes with a romanian gypsy in a shitty hotel in the suburbs

kys

62366054
>Women or leveraged, margined silver miners?
Mmmmm...... you're gonna have to get back to me on that bro

62366257
>>62365587
>>62365738
>>62366005
^ This system is outdated, but I've invented a new one. The equation is identical, but the compounding effects of the numerator have been upscaled, personality remains the same. Proximity now has a negative and positive value.

62366581
>>62366052
Yep, sounds incredibly based

62366590
>>62365989
Delusion
Just look at families walking around

62366599
>>62365958
>>62365953
Do you know any married men
And those things can be deal breakers, there are lines/limits

62366630
>>62365540
the only girls that care about careers in 2026 are egg freezing hags. you need just enough money to get to where women are (like the bar or anywhere else) and live somewhere. anything past that is diminishing returns.

62366635
anon don't listen to desperate insecure virgins for dating advice
the world is full of poor women who will date you if you have a good personality
when my wife and I started dating, I was literally unemployed and living with my parents
Now I make more than her but it took me 5 years to get there.

62366639
>>62365555
>I got a bluray player recently and im building up a collection

Dad?

62366677
>>62365608
all of these women made a different man spend thousands and wait months before fucking really makes you ponder

62366728
>>62366599
well eventually these kinds of women also tend to be single mothers

62367007
>>62365897
Yes paradoxically the girls that are most wife material aren't the ones going around looking to date. I think if you are out of school you're out of luck
>Girlfriend through connections
I know this sounds stupid, but I'd feel like a cuck if I met my girlfriend through a mutual.
Think about it, MY girlfriend would have been interested in talking to someone else FIRST. I wouldn't want to date a girl with guy friends anyways

Impossible propositions are abound

62367017
>>62366635
Do the math.

62367050
>>62367007
Yeah, that’s pretty retarded.
There are plenty of non-sexual reasons why a guy might know some other girl, like a family member, girlfriend/wife’s friend, former co-worker, former school friend.
You could make friends with girls to try to date one of their friends too.
The crux is on your ability to be a normal person to any normal person regardless of gender or age.
That’s how you find girls who aren’t sluts and divorce rapists. Not how you sleep around though.
So you have to figure out if what you really want is someone to have sex with regularly, or someone to start a family with. Because it’s a lot of social effort.

62367055
>>62367007
I just realized, you may have severely low self esteem if you can’t handle the thought of a gf talking to someone else before you.
Low self esteem is girl repellant
Fix that first

62367080
>>62365555
Checked and damn feels bad, I hope you make it.

>>62365540
Former sex haver here, if she loves you not much. Unironically you could go have a picnic with some bullshit like pizza, or go on a road trip. Strategic use of money is ideal. Don't just buy flowers, get them delivered to her work so all her coworkers get pissed and jealous. You want enough money to lock down a nice wedding and kids but thats really it, this was my fatal mistake. I have enough for three weddings now but opportunities are gone for me.

62367870
I get 1k CAD neetbux and do just fine
If not a demo thread, you're overthinking it retard

62367927
>>62365555
unironically, where are the lazy NEET gfs?

62368381
>>62365555
I just want to watch gun boat ball 2 wit my aspie unicorn believing gf turned housewife mommy with fear of scrutiny. I guess i was born too late

62368990
>>62367050
>>62367055
Think about it though. If a girl has tons of guy friends, that's a pretty clear no signal right?
>Low self esteem
true :(

item.title: So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. — 74 replies — 18.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo.

Open thread • Replies: 74 • Updated: 2026-06-15 03:27 UTC

OP

So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. How the fuck is it legal for them to destroy so much shareholder value? I WANT MY FUCKING MONEY BACK NOW!!

Early replies (first 3)

62348099
>>62348037
You did not lose anything, shoe shine boy. change your diapers and calm the FUCK down. Because you didn't sell yet. Right?

62348149
Better price to buy m8. You don't believe in the business anymore or somethin

62348170
If you couldn't tell the vidya industry is dead, I don't know what to tell you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62360459
>>62360284
Still better than Boomers and gen X ever were. And that is saying something. I have more in common with Zoomer 20 years younger than me than a lead maxed gen X who is 10 years older. Very refreshing to have lots more people to talk to without the lead brain damage. Haven't heard as many good ideas circulating widely until they grew up.

Seriously Gen X were scum who often didn't even give a shit their own kids needed to be potty trained until a kindergarten teacher told them they needed to be. Most of whom were totally normal kids of average intelligence they neglected that badly when it came to teaching them everyday things. This is who were most of the Zoomers parents. They could have turned out better if it weren't for that. Every other generation was horrified by 4 year olds still in diapers. They really didn't care like a drugged out hippy until the kid was old enough to go to public school.

62360515
>>62348037
No one guys Nintenslop anymore. Millennials are the last generation that cares about it, but are ageing out of gaming now.

62360525
You shouldnt buy a stock unless you plan on keeping it for 5 to 10 years

62360531
>>62360459
That's cool and all, but those fags will die off soon, and then they'll just end up shifting blame to kikes or anyone else besides themselves. Not to mention this faggy generation is just as tech illiterate and censorious as the boomers they bitch about.

62360534
0 mainline heavy hitters yet. Metroid was in dev hell for a decade.

62360549
>>62348278
>They are getting cooked by real handhelds now and I’m going to be buying another handheld isn’t a switch for my son.
Such as? The steam deck is now overpriced and other handheld PCs are even more expensive for similar offerings. So you're buying your son a system that's at least 2-3 more expensive than Nintendo's gaming system to play kid games that are available on every platform and even phones.... kek what a tard

62360629
>>62348278
>They are getting cooked by real handhelds now and I’m going to be buying another handheld isn’t a switch for my son
Making money out of handheld consoles depends on just 2 factors: How cheap can you make them and how much money can you get from software sales on that console.
Nintendo are the only handheld manufacturer with enough sales volume to get the best manufacturing prices. They are able to even commision custom silicon like Apple does with their phones to optimize things further, something that is completely out of reach of smaller players.
Nintendo also gets a ton of money from game sales and subscriptions as they limit things like the switch internet unless you pay and own game franchises that only release on their hardware.
Meanwhile their competitors need to spend more money to make an equivalent product and the only one that actually get revenue from software sales is Valve.
There's always going to be more powerful handhelds than whatever Nintendo makes as they don't design their stuff for performance, but there's not going to be a single handheld product that is as effective at generating net income as Nintendo's until someone starts getting closer to their sales volume.
Whenever Nintendo doesn't offer the best performance per dollar ratio, it is because they are selling their hardware at a premium due to how strong their brand is, not because they are incapable of making money at a lower pricepoint. Their competitors meanwhile are always running razor thin margins and any major impact to manufacturing costs needs an immediate readjustment to the sales price, as exemplified by the huge price bump the Deck got this month.

TL;DR: Nintendo doesn't make shit deals out of their products because they are incapable of offering something better, but because they have such an advantageous position that they don't need to. If someone ever gets close enough to force them to compete on prices/performance more, they will.

62360701
>>62360531
Millennials who hold good jobs are the most censorious. They were screened by Jewmers for that role. The rest found out recently that supporting that dick sucking networking will not give them high paying jobs if they didn't already have them. That door was already closed on them. With the last seats given out to the worst best friends of nepo baby Millennials they could find.

62360704
There was no normie hype for Switch 2. It was just more of the same. The writing was on the wall.

62361034
There was no normie hype for the iphone 6, or 7, or whatever number. It was more of the same. The writing was on the wall.

62361130
>picking stocks
Why do you retards keep doing this? You can't even have sex and by extension lack any social skills. You don't network. You don't get insider info. All the info and "intelligence" you get about companies and their future performance comes from mainstream sources like Bloomberg and Yahoo finance. You aren't built for trading or even decided where to invest.
Put your arrogance aside for a minute and start buying index funds or your destiny will be complete destitution and bitterness.

You have been warned.

62361211
>>62348170
>If you couldn't tell the vidya industry is dead
I don’t know the statistics behind it, but it sure does feel that way. Same with the movie industry. Nothing is like what it used to be.

62361368
>>62361211
Both the gaming and movie industry are dead. The level of creativity is long gone. Most of the best games that have ever existed are all games that came out early-mid 2000s and before. Most of the gaming that I do today is on the emulator. 8 and 16 bit games are timeless and, unlike 32/64 bit stuff, the graphics age well. It’s also quick to pick up and play, and they are simply fun. Same goes with arcade games. There’s some decent stuff on steam but a lot of creators simply rely on good graphics to carry their games. Besides, there’s only so many ideas that you can come up with for games before it gets old. All the genres have been rehashing the same type of games and all they’ve done is tweak the graphics and stories a bit. It’s unfortunate that newfags will never get to experience how fun the gaming atmosphere of the past used to be like.

62361513
>>62361130
I have made more money doing the opposite of what they tell me half the time. It just takes a bullshit detector skills to figure out if they are lying or not and looking at other company data they are ignoring. Sometimes I have noticed that Blackrock invests like low information investors and then tells normies to pump their bags. In fact just look to see how much mutual funds are already invested into a stock just ready to rug pull. At better strategy is to invest in other things and wait for them to bite. Then you rug pull them. Seriously I won big on investing in mining this way. Nearly everything I invested in they pumped once enough people familiar with the industry took notice. The scary thing is I am a collage dropout who never studied finance. Just self taught in my 30's and a little trial and error. Silver spoon nepo babies can just invest in a little of everything in a sector, hope for the best in a market cycle, and still make money.

62361516
Video gaming has collapsed and recovered multiple times but zoom zooms are too young to remember.

62361524
>>62361130
90% of my money is in boomer mutual funds and I take the remaining 10% and pick stocks. It isn't always one or the other.

62361639
>>62354555
Yeah I don't get people, when I become involved with a company I turn into something of a cheerleader. It's just fun cheering on SK even if I have never heard of them before.

62361715
People can barely afford groceries and you're gambling on bing bing bow companies? Read the room ffs..

62362175
>>62348037
Would have probably made more money on pokemon cards. Anyway dumb as fuck to bet on a company that only knows how remake games about some short fat italian plumber.

62363751
>>62348037
Did you sell?
You didn't lose anything yet if you are holding.
Nintendo will be fine in long term.
Eventually they have enough blockbuster titles and Switch 2 will sell like crazy.

62363794
Time to buy

62363812
>>62360701
>my two moms
Americans think that they aren't still fighting for this just because their noses aren't being rubbed in it anymore. Crazy.

62363883
>>62348037
even when the games industry is performing well (it definitely is not rn) it still sucks compared to other industries. i probably know games better than other industries but i still wouldn't touch it with a 10ft pole

62363911
>>62363812
The underlings supporting this for free are mostly Boomers at this point. No Kings rally was one giant Boomer rally. With an occasional Gen X and elder Millennial. Just about everyone else was obviously dragged there by older people. I would rather get a slow boomer stock where a boomer acked at the wrong time being the bagholder. And in this case it is mostly a matter of circumstance. Young normies aren't as insufferable anymore. These people have to be paid to do this in large numbers. But I would not mind knowing an infantile Millennial normie will be bag holding. Who were the last dick suckers to be rewarded generously. It is just that I would also hurt some zoomers in the process.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JYbgfgE96VI&list=FLkFEa2pg_tj954pX0TsPtzw&index=3
The youngest sort of people I don't mind bag holding. If they support this sort of crap that is. Not wishing death but they deserve to have less money from propping up a system that fucks me over.

62364621
>>62349911
>Yes, it's in the archives, likely with either Xenoblade pics or Rosalina pics.
kek

62364644
>>62362175
Mario isn't fat, he is big boned. Every videogame he was a cute chibi so you think that. Come to think of it, Mario is a big red bear and Luigi is a tall green candle stick. It's starting to make sense. It isn't Luigi's turn yet.

Oh I am probably getting banned for this. Enjoy the laugh while you can.

62367514
>>62361211
There is no passion in film or vidya except for small/indie level creators. All of the large firms are owned by money hungry frat bro types who bullied gamers and nerds in hs.

62367714
>>62357443
Then why did they go public?

62368452
>>62348037
返金不可

62368653
>>62368452
Use google translate. We got a Nintendo executive in here!

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 98 replies — 16.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 98 • Updated: 2026-06-15 01:33 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62354827
>>62339328
Anyone bullish on avalanche?

62355050
>>62339328
I sold when the Epstein files dropped. Feels good.

62355133
>>62354803
That’s the part a lot of people overlook. If TBVs actually work as intended, I think people are underestimating how much TVL native BTC use cases could pull in.

62355136
>>62355050
Time will tell whether it was the right call, but at least you acted according to your move.

62355617
>>62355133
It's crazy that DeFi security is still one of the biggest problems in the space.

62355631
>>62354827
Buy avax today and sell @ $100

62355632
>>62339417
Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
And monero is based on bitcoin. The entire crypto market exists because of all the money in bitcoin which is now backed by the US government.

62356269
>>62355617
At this point, thorough audits and multiple layers of review should be the standard, not a nice bonus anon.

62356538
>>62355631
What's your basis?

62357046
>>62345600
Probably the better route. We've seen too many centralized services blow up over the years, so you can't really blame people for preferring fully decentralized setups.

62357057
>>62355632
What about ZEC?

62357077
ICP

62357115
>>62339328
I sold at 116k I dont regret it at all and have about 2 mil waiting for sept-dec buy in.

62357126
>>62339328
I can never regret buying BTC desu, I bought back in around 70K and will DCA more if price goes below 60K. I'm also staking a little BTC on babylon for some extra yield

62357923
>>62355632
>Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
yeah just like the bitcoin developers right? they're the ones who control bitcoin?

it's a good question though, what if they were? miners would have to agree on consensus on which code to accept. if github ever refused to host monero's code then it would just move to .onion or .i2p

62359525
>>62357046
That's how it goes. They've already got GoMining, Ledger, and a16z involved, so let's see who they bring in next.

62359541
>>62357057
ZEC is interesting. If the privacy narrative picks up again, it's usually one of the first names people bring up.

62359557
>>62339328
I literally don't care about it. Mogged by virtually everything else over the last couple of years.

Also why is faggot shit like dogecoin still worth billions of dollars.

>in b4 bottom signal
Probably.

62359588
>>62357126
Sounds like you've got a plan and you're sticking to it. Hard to ask for much more than that. I wouldn't be surprised if more people follow suit over time. The $4B+ TVL shows there's already plenty of interest.

62359608
>>62359557
The market has a weird habit of rewarding the things nobody can rationally explain.

62359673
>>62357077
People still remember the launch, but the tech discussion around ICP is often more interesting than the price chart.

62360479
>>62359557
When bottom

62361782
>>62360479
Nobody knows

62362657
>>62359557
How do you mean?

62362661
>>62339328
While BTC is in deep bear stage, it's time to get in. I need to be greedy when others are fear.

62363445
>>62362657
It's his opinion

62364599
>>62339349
>Time to start acting like an african american.
finally someone said it

62365449
>>62362661
You're just doing the right thing..

62365460
any time you can cash out, turn a profit and not get anally blasted by exchanges is a good time to cash out.

62368338
>>62364599
Damn

item.title: Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money? — 100 replies — 11.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money?

Open thread • Replies: 100 • Updated: 2026-06-14 22:45 UTC

OP

>Tesla makes $3.9B/year, and the amount goes down year after year, yet has a 1.5T market cap
>SpaceX literally loses money and its claim to future profit is Grok, a cash-burning AI model that is losing to GPT and Claude
>Twitter is breakeven at best
What's going on here? Are white boomers just throwing money at him because they don't like seeing Indians, Chinese and Jews running shit in tech? This is the only explanation for the Elon Musk phenomenon I can make any sense of.

Early replies (first 3)

62361459
>>62361452
People rightly see him as a visionary for the future of humanity with the ability to actually do the stuf he foresees.

on the negative side much of what he envisions is distopian

and the first person to pioneer a technology is often NOT the person who gets rich off it

but you have to start somewhere, and he certainly does stuff other companies can't.

62361461
companies do not have to be profitable for people to speculate on them performing well in the future, and it helps when everyone said every company would go to zero but instead became some of the most valuable assets by marketcap in the world because they provided goods and services people wanted

62361471
>>62361459
>rightly
He hasn't even done anything, period.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62364330
>>62361452
Hypeman incarnated.

62364434
>>62364215
Sometimes I forget how retarded people on this board can be. Thanks for the reminder

62364440
>>62364434
It's genuinely fucked how antisemitic this board can be. Why Mossad hasn't gotten it shut down and its posters dragged through legal coals is fucking mystifying to me.

62364443
>>62364188
Yeah people don't seem to get that the whole point of the company is to offer a form of immortality down the road, by creating neural-digital interfacing that can bridge consciousness between brain and computer. Neuralink can and will charge any amount they want for that kind of tech, once it matures. They could price it at ten billion 2026 dollars per person and they would have a years or even decades-long waitlist.

62364868
>>62364443
yeah anon when moon

62364886
>>62361471
keep seething commie

62364900
>>62363679
Most people talking about him are dogshit retarded

62364907
SpaceX is being destroyed because Elon has to pay off billions of debt for his AI shit and Twitter purchase. The easiest way to do this without destroying Tesla was to merge them all together and IPO them. They're going to sell their stock after Blackrock and Vanguard dump the boomer 401k money buying them

62364918
>>62361478
He threw a pretty good fastball that one time. It made more progress than the vehicle did thoughbeit.

62365050
>>62361452
because stocks don't behave rationally and memes move the market more strongly than financial schizobabble

62365078
>>62361452
you can become one too, just create a crypto coin, mint 1T tokens and sell 1 for $1 on the open markets

62365099
>>62361452
heres the thing. it was all vaporware the whole time...tesla included. it just wasnt as brazen as the ipo before...worse with every scam. so he could keep borrowing. because hes doing "buy borrow die", it became the banks problem at this point because hes never gonna pay them back, he couldnt if he wanted to, he couldnt if they tried to liquidate. they are heavily incentivized to help him dump the baggies on you to close out these transactions. then he can have a fake ketamine overdose and go party with jeffjeff again.

62365104
>>62361452
elon is one of the only men actually creating value in this world.

almost the entire economy is pure pointless bullshit
>delivering sneakers to bestial niggers
>selling sloppa to bestial niggers
>keeping morbidly obese 100 year old bestial niggers alive
>manufacturing weapons so bestial niggers can shoot each other and blow shit up
>making handbags for the bestial femboons

elon is pursuing the future, the transcendence. he deserves to win.

62365105
>>62365104
They are going to deepfake it all just like nasa with kubrik except this time you paid for it by buying the ipo instead of pillaged wwii wealth

62365111
>>62365105
you are simply a low iq schizoid. you're exactly like the npcs except you're against the current thing.

62365115
>>62365111
You got mkultrad because your uncle molested you while you watched star trek or some other faggot shit and you liked it. Now spaecekeks launches make you feel cummies

62365124
>>62365115
very complex projection. must be something personal.

62365130
>>62365124
Keep fantacizing about scifi bullshit that will never happen. I hope you buy a neuralink and end up like terry schiavo

62365216
>>62365130
low iq npcs such as yourself cannot conceive of something which is not the current thing. you have my pity.

62365235
>>62361452
vibe investing.

62365240
>>62361452
it's because Musk is a cult leader

62365247
>>62361459
Literally what is "dystopian" about swarms of AI powered assassin drones?

62366996
>>62361471
I wish I could make a trillion doing nothing

62367047
Space X is worth 2 trillion...because it just is ok!

62367049
>>62363603

62367163
>>62361452
Elon Cuck

62367477
>>62361549

62367557
every major technological transformation in last 15 years is due to elon.

electric cars = tesla = elon
biohacking = neuralink = elon
space and global broadban = spacex = elon
fixing transporation = boring = elon
AI = openAI (elon founded it) + grok = elon
social media = X = elon

that's why he's a trillionaire and ur a nobody posting a forum when you should be grinding like elon who regularily sleeps on factory floors.

62367687

62367701
